reviews.title: I feel a disparity in hospitality.
reviews.text: While I often stayed at Shangri-La hotels overseas, this time I chose the Grand Mercure for the first time and was looking forward to it. I wanted to visit the night market for sightseeing, and when I inquired about it, I was met with a reaction of, “There?! Really?!” Additionally, when I wanted to go to a local shopping center for market research, the response was along the lines of, “You want to go there?!” I expected the hotel to cater to affluent guests, but at Shangri-La, I never experienced such reactions. The staff by the pool and facilities were very kind and treated everyone equally.
reviews.response:
Dear Ms. Fukiko,

Thank you for your feedback. We sincerely apologize for any shortcomings you experienced during your stay. Your comments are invaluable in helping us improve, and we are committed to addressing the issues you raised to ensure a better experience for all our guests. We hope to have the opportunity to welcome you back and provide you with the exceptional stay you deserve.

Best regards,
Hannah Phan
Guest Experience Manager

reviews.title: Wonderful hotel in good location but noisy presential sweet with suboptimal cleaning.
reviews.positives:
This was our last stop before leaving charming Viet Nam, we booked a grand deluxe king room on a high floor but were pleasantly surprised with upgrade to the presidential suite, where even toilets have a remote control.
The hotel location is a walking distance from West Lake but area around hotel is still under development, so we were brave to walk back at night only once. The hotel staff are very helpful and shared with us secrets of the best shopping locations in Hanoi. 
The hotel offers delicious breakfast with wonderful selection of dishes, able to satisfy most of the travelers.
reviews.negatives:
The presidential suit is one floor below lounge, which is a bit of mistake in design, in my humble opinion. At night people singing and dancing, from the early morning cleaning staff moving the furniture and vacuum cleaning – no rest for the wicked.  We are actually asked for a quiet room at the time of the booking!
Other minor comment is that water in the shower is very weak and half of the nozzles are blocked. The hotel staff proudly puts cards with the their names after the cleaning but I think there is a room for improvement there.

reviews.title: Beautiful swimming pool! The room is good too.
reviews.text: The pool view is pretty and looks great in photos. The rooftop is spacious and beautiful, but perhaps because it was daytime, they didn't sell drinks or beer. reviews.title: A mixed experience at a new Indochinese hotel
reviews.text: This hotel offers a unique Indochinese experience, with its eco-friendly design and cultural decoration. The facilities are modern and comfortable, and the staff are courteous and attentive. The food is tasty and varied, reflecting the local cuisine. The only downside was the internet speed, which was very slow and unreliable. We could not watch any YouTube videos without buffering. The breakfast service was also disappointing, as the food ran out quickly and the labels were incorrect. Another thing that bothered us was that they did not offer complimentary coffee, even though it was a 5-star hotel. We had to pay extra if we wanted to use the coffee cubes in the room. This hotel has a lot of potential, but it needs some improvement.

reviews.title: great new hotel in Hanoi, I was impressed
reviews.text:
Impressed with this new hotel in Hanoi.

Service - excellent. I had numerous phone and email exchanges, multiple changes to the booking, all dealt with efficiently and to my satisfaction. During the stay, the staff were very helpful and polite, both front desk, exec lounge, and during breakfast.

Rooms - brand new, and luxurious. Quiet too, despite being in the middle of the city (albeit I had a 14th floor room, so maybe that's why). They were, however, a bit smaller than I would expect. Especially as we had the Executive King rooms, which are meant to be a few levels up from the base rooms.

Exec Lounge - beautiful views, good service. Afternoon tea was a bit so-so; the quality of the food could be better, and maybe more choice. Drinks and overall service was great though.

Breakfast - great selection of foods, albeit little changes from day to day, so not one to stay for longer than maybe 3-4 days, if you like variety.

Pool - very disappointing? Only a rooftop pool. Small, with hardly a view from the pool itself. And it looked half-filled when we went to inspect. No-one swimming, no bar, etc there.

Location - great location, right next to the Temple of Literature. Possibly walking distance to the old town, but Grab taxis were like 35k-50k only to most places we needed to go.

Overall, a good hotel. I was impressed. Especially as the rates were around USD150/nt for Executive Rooms with breakfast.

Thực sự, mọi trải nghiệm của gia đình tôi ở khách sạn Grand Mercure Hanoi là rất tuyệt vời.

reviews.response:
Kính gửi Quý khách,
Chúng tôi chân thành cảm ơn Quý khách đã chia sẻ trải nghiệm tại Grand Mercure Hanoi. Rất tiếc khi biết rằng thiết kế ánh sáng và hệ thống điện trong phòng chưa mang lại sự thoải mái như Quý khách mong đợi.
Hệ thống điện thông minh được chúng tôi lắp đặt với mục đích mang lại sự tiện lợi và trải nghiệm hiện đại hơn cho khách lưu trú. Tuy nhiên, thật đáng tiếc khi trong trường hợp này, hệ thống đã không đáp ứng được kỳ vọng của Quý khách và gây ảnh hưởng đến giấc ngủ. Chúng tôi đã ghi nhận phản hồi của Quý khách để kiểm tra, điều chỉnh nhằm tránh tình huống tương tự xảy ra.
Một lần nữa, xin cảm ơn Quý khách đã dành thời gian chia sẻ và mong sẽ có cơ hội được chào đón Quý khách trở lại với trải nghiệm tốt hơn.
Trân trọng,
Nguyen The Quang
Front Office Manager
Grand Mercure Hanoi

reviews.title: Very good
reviews.positives: The staff were super friendly and very helpful

reviews.title: Khách sạn đẹp, đồ ăn sáng ok
reviews.text:
Mình đi gia đình có 2 ngày nghỉ ở đây. Điểm ấn tượng của mình là khá ok.
- Phòng ok nhưng hơi nhỏ so với mình nghĩ, đồ dùng đầy đủ, rất xịn nhưng điểm buồn nhất ko có bồn tắm là điểm khó hiểu khi ở KS 5*, mình đi 5* giá thường trên 2tr hết thì 100% đều có bồn tắm, nên lần này đặt mình bỏ sót cái này. Làm con mình không có chổ tắm. 
- Bể bơi 1.4m là thấy chịu rồi, nhỏ, 1.4m thì 2 đứa nhỏ mình chịu, ko xài đc, ko có bồn tắm, bể bơi lại nhỏ ko có khu vực cho trẻ em.
Đồ ăn sáng với mình thì ổn, ko nhiều nhưng ăn ok, nhưng 2 ngày ko đổi món nhiều.
Ngoài những cái trên thì còn lại mình đều ok.
reviews.response:
Anh Tùng thân mến,

Cám ơn anh Tùng đã giành thời gian chia sẻ về trải nghiệm của mình tại khách sạn Grand Mercure Hanoi.
Khách sạn Grand Mercure Hanoi rất tiếc vì một số điểm chưa đạt được như mong muốn của anh Tùng và gia đình, chúng tôi trân thành xin lỗi về sự bất tiện đó
Tại Grand Mercure Hanoi chúng tôi luôn cố gắng không ngừng nâng cao chất lượng dịch vụ của mình.
Rất mong sớm được đón anh và gia đình quay lại khách sạn Grand Mercure Hanoi và có những trải nghiệm tốt hơn

Trân trọng
Nguyễn Thế Quang
Front Office Manager
